mission_mom.label: Mission-driven mom
mission_mom.description: Family group of 2-4 including 1-2 kids. Diwali-outfit-for-self-and-kids mission. Mostly arrives morning to beat the evening crowd.

arrival_minute_dist.note: Mode ~11am, mean ~12pm. Drives the morning peak (11am-1pm).

dwell_minute_dist.note: Mode ~57 min, mean ~57 min. Efficient but multiple try-ons.

young_woman.label: Browse-and-buy young woman
young_woman.description: Solo or with a friend. Heavy trial-room use, longer dwell. Responds to power wall freshness. Zudio-poacher segment.

family_weekend.label: Family weekend shopper
family_weekend.description: Group of 4-6, multi-generational, Diwali-and-wedding-season mode. Largest basket, highest absolute spend. AC-sensitive.

arrival_minute_dist.note: Mode ~5pm, mean ~7pm. Drives the evening peak.

dwell_minute_dist.note: Mode ~65 min, mean ~85 min. Big group, slow.

quick_trip_male.label: Quick-trip men's wear
quick_trip_male.description: Solo male, often dropped off by family. Minimal trial. Queue-intolerant.

browser.label: Browser, no purchase intent
browser.description: Walks 1-2 zones, walks out. Influenced by power wall. ~3-5% convert opportunistically under Mahabachat.

arrival_minute_dist.note: Spread across day with slight afternoon bias. Wider df = more spread.

dwell_minute_dist.note: Mode ~3 min, mean ~11 min. Many walk out under 5 min.

notes_on_chi_square: Each arrival/dwell distribution is sampled as `chi2(df) * scale + shift`, then clipped to [min, max]. Chi-square with df=k has mode max(0, k-2) and mean k. So mode in minutes ≈ (k-2)*scale + shift and mean ≈ k*scale + shift. Adjust df to control spread (higher df = wider tail), scale to compress/stretch, shift to anchor.

qualitative_check.morning_peak_11am_1pm_arrivals: mostly mission_mom + a few young_woman, browser tail
qualitative_check.lunch_dip_2pm_4pm: tails only, no profile centered here
